medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Posterior distal radius swelling, increasing in size with growth. Angular deformity related to intra-articular lesions of the wrist and knee.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Negative family history of bone dysplasia.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Posterior distal radius lesion leading to immediate instability of the wrist. Lesion of carpal ligaments and dorsal capsular distension.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Asymmetrical epiphyseal overgrowth and exostoses of osteochondroma-like appearance. Irregular intra-articular mass with fragmented asymmetrical ossification arising from either the lateral or frequently the medial half of the affected epiphysis without metaphyseal changes.
Clear cleavage plane between the epiphysis and the abnormal cartilaginous growth.

Early diagnosis of DEH before the onset of ossification. Defines the intra-articular osteocartilaginous mass, its extension and anatomical relations. Provides detailed images of associated joint deformity. Shows a clear cleavage plane between the epiphysis and the pathological tissue.

other_tests.findings: Cartilaginous nodules with osseous metaplasia, like those of an osteochondroma picture. After complete ossification, the lesion is microscopically indistinguishable from an osteochondroma: a base of normal bone and a cap of hyaline cartilage are observed.

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on clinical findings of a swelling bony mass, imaging studies (plain radiographs, CT scans, and MRI) showing asymmetrical epiphyseal overgrowth and exostoses of osteochondroma-like appearance, and histological confirmation of cartilaginous nodules with osseous metaplasia. MRI was particularly useful in identifying a cleavage plane between the epiphysis and the pathological tissue, guiding surgical excision.
